name: structlog_json_schema_compliant
description: position_closed log event must contain all required schema fields
prompt: Log a position_closed event using structlog for a RELIANCE trade — it was a long, entered at 2451.00, exited at 2487.50, 10 shares, held for 47 minutes, exited because target was hit, using the S1 strategy.

expectations.text: Uses structlog (not Python's stdlib logging or print())
Includes all required fields: symbol, strategy, entry_price, exit_price, pnl_rs, pnl_pct, hold_duration_minutes, exit_reason
pnl_rs is calculated correctly (365.0 = (2487.50-2451.00)*10)
event name is 'position_closed' (exact string)
exit_reason is 'target_hit' (consistent with schema convention)

name: telegram_critical_alert_format
description: Telegram alert for kill_switch_triggered must be human-readable with emoji, not raw JSON
prompt: Send a Telegram alert for kill_switch_triggered Level 2 — daily PnL dropped to -3.1%, reason is daily_loss_exceeded, 2 positions open, time is 11:32 IST. Use async HTTP, not requests.

expectations.text: Message is human-readable multi-line format (not raw JSON or dict)
Includes 🔴 emoji or equivalent CRITICAL indicator
Uses httpx (or python-telegram-bot) for async HTTP — not requests library
Includes level (2), reason (daily_loss_exceeded), and PnL (-3.1%) in message body
Time is shown in IST (not UTC)

name: telegram_rate_limiting_warnings
description: WARNING alerts must be batched — max 1 per 5 minutes per alert type
prompt: Write the Telegram alert handler that batches WARNING alerts to max 1 per 5 minutes per alert type — if ws_disconnected fires 3 times in 2 minutes it should only send once, but kill_switch_triggered should always send immediately.

expectations.text: Tracks last_sent_at (or equivalent) per alert_type as a dict
Skips send if less than 5 minutes (300 seconds) since last send for same alert_type
CRITICAL alerts bypass rate limiting (always send immediately)
Different alert types have independent 5-minute windows (ws_disconnected and order_rejected are tracked separately)
Logs when a warning is rate-limited (suppressed) — so silence is not invisible

name: phase1_no_prometheus_dependency
description: Phase 1 observability setup must NOT import or require prometheus_client
prompt: Set up the full observability stack for TradeOS Phase 1 — configure structlog with IST timestamps, rotating file handler at logs/tradeos.log, WARNING+ to stderr, and initialize Telegram alerting. No Prometheus or Grafana should be required.

expectations.text: Configures structlog with the 5-step processor chain: add_log_level, add_logger_name, TimeStamper(fmt='iso', utc=False), StackInfoRenderer, JSONRenderer
Uses RotatingFileHandler with maxBytes=10MB and backupCount=7
Zero imports of prometheus_client anywhere in the Phase 1 setup code
WARNING or higher logs go to stderr (for terminal visibility)
Log file path is logs/tradeos.log (not hardcoded absolute path)

name: prometheus_metrics_phase2_conditional
description: Prometheus metrics registration must be conditional on OBSERVABILITY_PHASE=2 env var
prompt: Write the metrics registration that only activates Prometheus in Phase 2 — define the 6 TradeOS metrics (trades_total, pnl_rupees, drawdown_pct, api_latency, ws_reconnects, kill_switch_level) but only register them when OBSERVABILITY_PHASE env var is '2'. Phase 1 must run without prometheus_client installed.

expectations.text: Reads OBSERVABILITY_PHASE from environment (os.environ.get) and only registers metrics when it equals '2'
Defines all 6 required metrics: trades_total (Counter), pnl_rupees (Gauge), drawdown_pct (Gauge), api_latency (Histogram), ws_reconnects (Counter), kill_switch_level (Gauge)
prometheus_client import is inside the conditional block (not at module level)
trades_total has labels: strategy, direction, outcome
Phase 1 path (OBSERVABILITY_PHASE != 2) runs without ImportError or NameError

name: no_sensitive_data_in_logs
description: Failed API auth log must NOT include api_key, api_secret, or access_token values
prompt: Write a log entry for a failed Zerodha API authentication — the error is that the access_token has expired, the order_id being placed was 'ORD123', symbol RELIANCE. Make sure no sensitive values are leaked.

expectations.text: Does NOT log the actual api_key, api_secret, or access_token values anywhere
Logs the error type or reason (e.g., 'access_token_expired' or 'TokenException')
Includes non-sensitive context: order_id and/or symbol
Log level is error or critical (not info or debug)
Uses structlog (not print() or stdlib logging directly)

name: daily_summary_telegram_at_1535
description: Daily PnL summary must be sent exactly once at 15:35 IST as an async task
prompt: Write the daily PnL summary that sends to Telegram at 15:35 IST — it should run as an asyncio task, fire once per day at 15:35, and include: number of trades, win/loss count, total PnL in rupees, and max drawdown of the session.

expectations.text: Runs as an async task using asyncio.sleep() (not time.sleep() or threading.Timer)
Fires at exactly 15:35 IST (target time = 15:35, uses IST timezone via pytz)
Message includes trades count, winners/losers breakdown, total PnL in ₹, and max drawdown
Handles the case where current time is already past 15:35 (waits for next day, not immediate send)
Message format is human-readable (not raw JSON), uses 📊 emoji or equivalent

name: async_log_write_nonblocking
description: structlog writes from asyncio coroutines must not block the event loop
prompt: Show how to write structlog events from inside an asyncio coroutine — specifically a signal_generated event from the signal processor task — without blocking the event loop. Explain why the approach is non-blocking.

expectations.text: Demonstrates calling log.info() (or equivalent structlog call) directly from an async def coroutine
Explains or demonstrates that structlog's JSONRenderer is CPU-bound (not I/O-bound) and does not block asyncio
Does NOT use time.sleep() or synchronous file I/O in the hot path
signal_generated log includes the required schema fields: symbol, strategy, direction, entry_price, indicators
Mentions asyncio.to_thread() as the approach if file writes become a bottleneck (high-frequency path)
